expectations.text: A faithful benchmark requires the live appsmith-v2 repository checked out as the working codebase; the run cannot reuse the EXISTS findings, add code at the MISSING extension points, run the architecture check, or produce real commits without it, and the run states this dependency rather than fabricating file contents.
expectations.evidence: combined:     https://github.com/appsmithorg/appsmith-v2/pull/11556

expectations.text: Implements the scenarios in plan order (S1 then S2 then S3) and never starts a later scenario before the earlier one is committed.
expectations.evidence: report/plan: | 1 | S1 | Admin invites a new member by email    | researched |

expectations.text: Builds each scenario as a vertical end-to-end slice spanning every layer it touches (route, service, model/migration) so the scenario actually works for a user — it does NOT implement a single layer in isolation or batch all the database work first.
expectations.evidence: patch/report: +- `backend/app/routes/sandbox_routes.py`

expectations.text: Reuses exactly what kite-research marked EXISTS at the stated locations — require_workspace_admin (guards.py:88), enqueue_email (email_jobs.py:31), and WorkspaceMembershipService.add_member (workspace_membership.py:54) — instead of re-discovering or reimplementing them.
expectations.evidence: combined: - RQ1 → EXISTS: require_workspace_admin in backend/app/auth/guards.py:88 —

expectations.text: Adds new code only at the MISSING extension points research identified — the invitations model and migration, the partial unique index on (workspace_id, lower(email)) WHERE status = pending, and the new accept-invite route — rather than re-deciding where things should live.
expectations.evidence: patch/report: evidence not found

expectations.text: Tests selectively at the boundaries that matter — the S1 admin-authorization boundary, the S2 atomic single-membership-on-double-accept data-integrity boundary, and the S3 idempotent re-invite behavior — and does NOT generate exhaustive or mechanical tests for every implementation detail.
expectations.evidence: patch/report: +- `backend/tests/test_services/test_e2b_service_retry.py`

expectations.text: Runs the kite-arch-compass architecture check on each slice before committing it, and fixes any flagged violation before the commit rather than carrying it into the commit.
